title: The Markov blanket trick: On the scope of the free energy principle and active inference.

assets.abstract: The free energy principle (FEP) has been presented as a unified brain theory, as a general principle for the self-organization of biological systems, and most recently as a principle for a theory of every thing. Additionally, active inference has been proposed as the process theory entailed by FEP that is able to model the full range of biological and cognitive events. In this paper, we challenge these two claims. We argue that FEP is not the general principle it is claimed to be, and that activ

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Dependent arising — predictive processing, free energy principle, active inference as causal cognition.
